title: Ramondia palmata
taxonomy: Ramondia palmata is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. It belongs to the subfamily Rosoideae, and its genus Ramondia is relatively small, consisting of only a few species. The species R. palmata is native to the Iberian Peninsula, specifically in the mountainous regions of Spain and Portugal. Ramondia palmata is a type of shrub, and its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its compound leaves and showy white flowers. As a member of the Rosaceae family, R. palmata is closely related to other flowering plants like roses and strawberries.
